title: Our Honest Review: The Summer I Turned Pretty Season 3
text: The Summer I Turned Pretty Season 3 premiered July 16, with new episodes coming out each Wednesday. The Season 3 premiere pulled in around 25 million viewers in its first week, making it one of Prime Video's most successful releases. Reviews of Season 3 have been mixed and divided. Several key themes emerge: Pacing Issues - the season is terribly uneven, alternating between being a drag and rushing through key moments. Despite having 3 more episodes than usual, the first half of the season drags, spending entire episodes on moments that could have been a quick montage. Character Development Concerns - by having a full three-year jump without any meaningful change in the characters and their relationships, it makes everyone feel emotionally stunted past the point of believability. The acting stood out more than ever, especially from Lola Tung (Belly) and Christopher Briney (Conrad), with their emotional scenes giving real weight to the story. Overall it's not perfect but still delivers an emotional and dramatic finale that captures the ups and downs of young love, despite some pacing issues and significant fan backlash.
